element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Highest jump ability; default character for rescue mission
Allows selection of Dizzy Devil, Furrball, or Plucky Duck with unique abilities
Spin mode destroys walls and most enemies; required for specific passages
Climbing ability and slow descent; enables vertical exploration
Brief flight and superior swimming; navigates water and air gaps
30 carrots = 1 extra life from Hamton; resource management mechanic
Must exit door without being grabbed or world restarts; checkpoint/punishment mechanic
Character-specific strategies required; climax of each world
Single level format; escalated difficulty; themed environment
Single level format; final confrontation with Montana Max; story climax

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching safe platforms after hazard zones
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Three-level escalation per world (safe → avoidance → boss fight) with progressive difficulty. Worlds 5-6 compress tension into single-level formats, increasing density.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Star ball pickups signal character switching opportunities; Hamton location indicates carrot trading hub

difficulty_curve.early_game: World 1 (The Hills): Tutorial-paced platforming with basic navigation. Introduces Buster's jump-based toolset and carrot collection.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Worlds 2-4 (Wetlands, Trees, Downtown): Character-specific puzzles emerge. Elmyra encounters enforce pattern learning. Boss battles require ability selection. Spatial complexity increases with vertical navigation.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Worlds 5-6 (Wackyland, Monty's Mansion): Compressed single-level design concentrates all mechanics. Escalated enemy density and hazard combinations. Final boss confrontation demands mastery of all character abilities.
notable_spikes: First Elmyra encounter (World 1, Level 2) – introduces punishment mechanic
First boss battle (World 1, Level 3) – pattern recognition required
World 2-4 transitions – character switching becomes mandatory for progression
World 5 (Wackyland) – single-level format removes safety valve of multi-level pacing
World 6 (Monty's Mansion) – final gauntlet combining all introduced mechanics

notable_level_patterns.description: Three-level progression: Level 1 introduces world theme and basic challenges. Level 2 features Elmyra avoidance in enclosed arena (world-reset penalty). Level 3 concludes with boss battle. Character switching via star balls enables world-specific solutions.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any of Worlds 1-4 (Hills, Wetlands, Trees, Downtown)

notable_level_patterns.description: Worlds 5-6 collapse three-level structure into one. Removes intermediate relief, increasing tension density. All mechanics (platforming, character abilities, boss combat) occur within single session.
Enclosed area in Level 2 of each world. Player must navigate space avoiding NPC while finding exit. Capture forces world restart (harsh penalty). Creates pattern-learning checkpoint before boss.

Level 3 of each world. Boss design rewards character selection from earlier levels. Dizzy for wall-breaking, Furrball for vertical access, Plucky for mobility. Buster provides raw jump height advantage.
